passages.text: The Industrial Revolution was not merely a technological shift (8) ---- a profound socio-economic transformation. It (9) ---- the fundamental structures of European society, replacing agrarian traditions with urban lifestyles. While some historians (10) ---- the era as a period of unprecedented progress, others emphasize the (11) ---- living conditions of the working class. Regardless of one's stance, it is (12) ---- that this era laid the groundwork for modern capitalism.

The concept of 'biodiversity' encompasses the variety of all life on Earth. Currently, anthropogenic activities are accelerating extinction rates at an alarming pace. Habitat destruction remains the primary driver of this loss, particularly in tropical rainforests where specialized species cannot adapt to sudden changes. Conservationists argue that protecting ecosystem services is vital not only for wildlife but for human survival, as we rely on these systems for clean air and water.

In the digital age, the phenomenon of 'echo chambers' has become a significant concern for democratic discourse. Algorithms on social media platforms prioritize engagement, often feeding users content that aligns with their existing beliefs. This reinforcement of cognitive bias can lead to increased political polarization. To mitigate this effect, digital literacy programs must teach individuals how to seek out diverse perspectives and evaluate the credibility of varied information sources.

questions.question: The newly proposed environmental regulations are expected to have a(n) ---- effect on the reduction of carbon emissions in the next decade.

options.B: substantial
options.C: infrequent
options.D: redundant

questions.question: Teknolojideki hızlı gelişmeler, eğitim sistemlerinin öğrencileri geleceğin iş dünyasına hazırlama şeklini temelden değiştirmektedir.

options.A: Rapid advances in technology are fundamentally changing the way education systems prepare students for the future business world.
options.B: The way education systems prepare students for the future has changed due to rapid technological advances.
options.C: Education systems must change their way of preparing students for the business world because of technology.
options.D: Technology is changing so rapidly that education systems can no longer prepare students for the future.
options.E: The future of the business world depends on how education systems adapt to technological advances.

questions.question: Fosil yakıtların aşırı kullanımı sadece çevresel bozulmaya yol açmakla kalmıyor, aynı zamanda halk sağlığını da ciddi şekilde tehdit ediyor.

options.A: Using fossil fuels excessively leads to environmental degradation and health problems.
options.B: The excessive use of fossil fuels not only leads to environmental degradation but also seriously threatens public health.
options.C: Public health and the environment are both threatened by the use of fossil fuels.
options.D: Fossil fuels are used so much that they threaten the environment and public health at the same time.
options.E: Not only the environment but also public health is damaged when we use fossil fuels excessively.

questions.question: Many scientists believe that artificial intelligence will eventually surpass human intelligence in most cognitive tasks.

options.A: Birçok bilim insanı, yapay zekanın sonunda çoğu bilişsel görevde insan zekasını geride bırakacağına inanıyor.
options.B: Yapay zekanın insan zekasını çoğu alanda geçeceği düşüncesi birçok bilim insanı tarafından desteklenmektedir.
options.C: Çoğu bilim insanına göre yapay zeka ve insan zekası bilişsel görevlerde bir gün yarışacaktır.
options.D: Yapay zekanın bilişsel görevlerde insanı geçmesi bilim dünyasında tartışılan bir konudur.
options.E: Bilim insanlarının çoğu, yapay zekanın insan zekasıyla aynı seviyeye geleceğine inanmaktadır.

questions.question: Although the government has taken several measures to reduce poverty, the income gap continues to widen.

options.A: Hükümet yoksulluğu azaltmak için önlemler alsa da gelir adaletsizliği artmaya devam ediyor.

options.E: Hükümetin önlemlerine rağmen yoksulluk ve gelir uçurumu arasındaki ilişki devam etmektedir.

options.B: The manager wanted to have the meeting on Monday regardless of his busy schedule.
options.C: Next Monday's meeting was cancelled because the manager had a conflict.
options.D: Because the manager had a conflict, he decided not to attend the meeting next Monday.

questions.question:

questions.question: Dark matter is one of the greatest mysteries of modern astrophysics. ----. Although it cannot be seen directly, its presence is inferred from its gravitational effects on visible matter, such as stars and galaxies. Without dark matter, galaxies would likely fly apart as they rotate.

options.B: The sun is the center of our solar system
options.C: NASA is planning a mission to Mars next year
options.D: Black holes are regions of space where gravity is very strong
options.E: Telescopes have become much more powerful recently
